title: Glistening Vietnamese Pork Sandwich
description: An earthy, springy, Whole30-friendly pork sandwich that bursts with poppy seeds and a deglaze fond, perfect for a lazy Sunday lunch.
instructions: Heat a skillet over medium-high and sear the pork strips until browned and cooked through, about 5 minutes, then set aside.
Deglaze the pan with 1 cup of low-sodium chicken broth, scraping up browned bits to form a fond.
Reduce the deglaze by half to concentrate flavor, about 3 minutes, and let cool slightly.
In a bowl, whisk fish sauce, lime juice, sesame oil, and a pinch of salt to create a quick vinaigrette.
Toss the cooked pork with the vinaigrette until evenly coated.
Toast the almond flour bun on a griddle until crisp on the edges, about 2 minutes.
Spread a thin layer of the cooled fond on the bun’s bottom half.
Layer the pork, carrot, cucumber, bamboo shoots, pickled daikon, and cilantro on top of the fond.
Sprinkle poppy seeds over the assembled sandwich.
Cover with the top half of the bun and press gently to seal.
Slice the sandwich in half and serve immediately for a crisp bite.
Optionally, drizzle a little extra fish sauce on the side for dipping.
